goal.answer: Implement Phase 3 (guided investigation loop) and Phase 4 (structured root-cause report) of the observability incident-triage agent. Transform the single-shot TriageService into a deterministic 5-step investigation state machine (Scope→Metrics→Traces→Logs→Hypothesize) that produces an auditable TriageReport with evidence, confidence scores, and Grafana deep links.

payload.plain: Implement Phase 3 (guided investigation loop) and Phase 4 (structured root-cause report) of the observability incident-triage agent. Transform the single-shot TriageService into a deterministic 5-step investigation state machine (Scope→Metrics→Traces→Logs→Hypothesize) that produces an auditable TriageReport with evidence, confidence scores, and Grafana deep links.

criteria.answer:
InvestigationStep interface and 5 concrete step classes exist and compile
InvestigationState captures each step's tool calls, LLM prompts/responses, and timestamps
TriageOrchestratorService runs the 5 steps sequentially in correct order
ScopeStep uses MCP discovery tools to identify relevant service and time window
MetricsStep invokes query_prometheus via Grafana MCP and extracts anomalous trace_ids
TraceStep invokes get_trace_by_id or search_traces via Jaeger MCP and identifies failing spans
LogStep invokes query_loki_logs via Grafana MCP filtered by trace_ids
HypothesisStep synthesizes evidence into ranked root causes with confidence scores
POST /api/triage/investigate returns structured TriageReport JSON with steps[], rootCause, confidence, deepLinks[], notChecked[]
JUnit integration test verifies the investigation loop executes all 5 steps in order and produces TriageReport
scripts/triage-agent-smoke-test.sh validates end-to-end flow and asserts structured response fields via jq

constraints.answer: Spring Boot 3.4, Java 17, Spring AI 1.1.4, Read-only by design — investigation must never invoke Grafana MCP write tools (Sift writes are already disabled by --disable-write), Preserve existing Docker observability network and service names (http://mcp-grafana:8000, http://jaeger-mcp-service:8080), Follow existing package conventions (com.example.triageagent) and Gradle patterns, OpenAiExtraBodyWorkaround required for Spring AI 1.1.4, Backward compatible: existing /api/triage/investigate endpoint evolves from string to structured JSON response

plain: Read-only by design — investigation must never invoke Grafana MCP write tools (Sift writes are already disabled by --disable-write)
Preserve existing Docker observability network and service names (http://mcp-grafana:8000, http://jaeger-mcp-service:8080)
Follow existing package conventions (com.example.triageagent) and Gradle patterns
Backward compatible: existing /api/triage/investigate endpoint evolves from string to structured JSON response

phaseSnapshots.name: Build triage playbook and report
phaseSnapshots.goal: Create the guided triage playbook and structured output layer.
phaseSnapshots.description: A single vertical slice covering step abstractions, 5 investigation step classes, orchestrator wiring, structured report DTOs, controller update, integration tests, and smoke-test script updates.

steps.description: Create InvestigationStep interface and InvestigationState (audit trail container). Create step classes: ScopeStep, MetricsStep, TraceStep, LogStep, HypothesisStep. Each step has a step-specific system prompt, target method reading/writing InvestigationState, and captured tool-call audit entry. Directory: triage-agent/src/main/java/com/example/triageagent/investigation/

verification.command: test -f triage-agent/src/main/java/com/example/triageagent/investigation/InvestigationStep.java && test -f triage-agent/src/main/java/com/example/triageagent/investigation/InvestigationState.java && test -f triage-agent/src/main/java/com/example/triageagent/investigation/ScopeStep.java && test -f triage-agent/src/main/java/com/example/triageagent/investigation/MetricsStep.java && test -f triage-agent/src/main/java/com/example/triageagent/investigation/TraceStep.java && test -f triage-agent/src/main/java/com/example/triageagent/investigation/LogStep.java && test -f triage-agent/src/main/java/com/example/triageagent/investigation/HypothesisStep.java

steps.description: Create TriageOrchestratorService that runs steps sequentially (scope→metrics→traces→logs→hypothesis). Inject ChatClient.Builder, ToolCallbackProviders, and a List<InvestigationStep>. Each step receives InvestigationState. Capture step name, tool calls, LLM prompts/responses, and timestamps for audit trail.

Create structured DTOs: InvestigationStepDTO, InvestigationStateDTO, TriageReport (rootCause, confidence, steps[], deepLinks[], notChecked[]). Add StepOutput class for captured tool results. Update TriageController to return ResponseEntity<TriageReport>.
Update controller and service wiring: ensure TriageController delegates to TriageOrchestratorService, returning TriageReport at POST /api/triage/investigate. Keep original endpoint path.

Add JUnit integration test mocking ChatClient to simulate step progression, verifying all 5 steps execute in order and populate InvestigationState with mock data. Add test verifying JSON serialization of TriageReport.
Update scripts/triage-agent-smoke-test.sh to add jq assertions verifying response contains .steps, .rootCause, .confidence. Run smoke test against live stack.
Run full build: ensure ./gradlew :triage-agent:build passes and docker compose config remains valid after all changes.

assumptions.answer: OpenAI API key supplied at runtime via OPENAI_API_KEY env var. Phase 0/1 services are running before the investigation loop executes (Prometheus, Loki, Jaeger, Grafana MCP, Jaeger MCP); Spring AI MCP client SSE connections default to /sse endpoint; Grafana MCP and Jaeger MCP tools match Phase 1 schema; Deep-link generation depends on discovered Grafana datasource UIDs and dashboard UIDs from the MCP discovery step; Step-level tool calling and LLM interpretation use the same ChatClient + OpenAiExtraBodyWorkaround pattern already proven in Phase 2; The investigation loop is deterministic — the 5-step sequence is hardcoded, though each step uses LLM guidance to interpret tool results

payload.summary: Phase 1 complete. The investigation loop (Scope→Metrics→Traces→Logs→Hypothesize) and structured root-cause report are fully implemented. All 5 step classes run sequentially via TriageOrchestratorService, producing a TriageReport with steps, rootCause, confidence, deepLinks, and notChecked. 15 JUnit tests pass, smoke test passes against live stack with real OpenAI tool calls returning a structured report with 5 investigation steps and confidence=0.8.

payload.finalSummary: Phase 3 and 4 complete. The triage-agent now runs a deterministic 5-step investigation state machine (Scope→Metrics→Traces→Logs→Hypothesize) and returns a structured TriageReport with evidence, confidence scores, Grafana deep links, and explicit not-checked items. The implementation includes InvestigationStep interface, 5 @Component step classes, TriageOrchestratorService with error resilience and timing capture, structured DTOs (TriageReport, InvestigationStepDTO, ToolCallDTO), InvestigationMapper, updated controller, 15 passing JUnit tests, and a passing smoke test against the live stack with real OpenAI tool calls.
